subproblems.statement: Does the published 2024-2026 LLM literature already cover a constraint between attention-head count and per-head effective rank/budget along a fixed log-log frontier?
If yes, what name does it go by — head pruning, mixture-of-heads, head-rank deflation, etc. — and does any of that work explicitly invoke a self-thinning / null-space constraint?
Is the Reineke-style log(density) + 1.605 * log(diameter) = constant frontier the functionally same constraint as the published head-budget-vs-effective-rank rule, or genuinely different?
